data.executiveSummary: The payment-api service experienced a series of GatewayExceptions due to an external API latency spike, which exhausted the connection pool, leading to increased error rates.

rootCause.title: External API Latency Spike
rootCause.description: The payment-api experienced exhaustion of connection pool due to latency spikes from an external API. This significantly impacted the service's ability to handle requests effectively.

evidenceChain: Step 1: Loki logs show numerous GatewayExceptions indicating 'External API latency spike detected. Connection pool exhausted.' across a 30-minute duration.
Step 2: Prometheus metrics show HTTP request duration indicates latency issues, with a large percentage of requests taking longer than expected.

timeline.event: GatewayException: External API latency spike detected. Connection pool exhausted.
Repeated GatewayException: External API latency spike detected.
Ongoing GatewayExceptions related to external API latency.

data.impact: The incident caused an increase in payment-api error rates to 4.79%, affecting transaction processing and potentially leading to a degraded customer experience.

similarIncident.title: Redis Connection Timeout Spikes
similarIncident.resolution: Restarted redis-primary-3 instance due to orphaned connections.

recommendedActions: Evaluate and potentially augment connection pool settings to better handle sudden external API latencies.
Consider implementing retries or fallbacks for critical external API interactions.
As per the resolution of KAN-6, consider investigating and restarting any affected instances to resolve connection pooling issues.
